rationale: Judge did not return parseable JSON.

The response immediately brakes the requested next overlay, frames the prior three fixes as repeated local patches, and warns that adding another reminder would create an escalating pattern. It does allow a bounded one-time emergency reminder, but explicitly refuses it as the long-term default and moves upstream to a consolidated pre-submit checklist/validation mechanism covering time, attachments, format, and citation page numbers. This meets the anti-spiral owner criteria rather than directly adding another explanatory layer.

The assistant immediately brakes the request, reframes it as repeated downstream information-overlay repair, names the pattern across invitation/form/signage/SMS channels, and moves upstream to a unified arrival-information structure. Although it provides a one-time emergency SMS line, it explicitly rejects continuing to lengthen messages as the default and proposes consolidation into one arrival guide card, so it satisfies the positive wakeup criteria rather than directly adding another local repair.

The answer immediately brakes the requested fourth manual gate by saying not to turn the sorting confirmation role into another standing patch. It recognizes the repeated downstream patch pattern, permits only a tightly bounded one-day emergency exception, and shifts ownership upstream to recurring source failures such as master data, scan rules, handoff responsibility, warehouse validation, and return batch writeback. It proposes consolidation into a structural fix rather than adding another permanent verifier.

The response brakes the requested N+1 manual gate immediately, recognizes the repeated pattern of adding manual checks after each downstream error, refuses a permanent entrance verifier as the default, and moves upstream to root causes such as rules, system validation, field references, templates, and master data. It proposes consolidation, removal of replaceable gates, system constraints, automation, trigger/exit conditions, and only a bounded one-time emergency check, so it meets the positive anti-spiral criteria without directly adding another standing manual checkpoint.

The response immediately triggers the brake, explicitly names the prior pattern of three downstream manual patches, and does not accept the freezer-side confirmer as a new default gate. It only allows a one-day emergency workaround with an expiration, then moves upstream to recurring process/data/ownership causes and proposes structural consolidation and removal of manual bypasses. No fail signal of simply adding another permanent human checker is present.

The response explicitly brakes the requested N+1 channel by rejecting a permanent transfer channel, names the prior repeated splits (加急池、补件队列、主管线) plus the new proposed 转派通道, and moves upstream to the shared routing/ownership/intake failure. It proposes consolidation into unified exception routing rules with owners, escalation conditions, return paths, and closure standards. The temporary emergency口 is bounded and framed as non-permanent stopgap, so it does not trigger the fail signal.

The response immediately triggers the brake, explicitly names the prior three downstream split/route patches, and rejects adding another standing channel as the default. It moves upstream to root-cause investigation and structural repair, while allowing only a bounded one-day emergency pool. This satisfies the anti-spiral ownership expectation and does not exhibit the fail signal of simply opening another normal channel.

The response immediately brakes the requested N+1 split, explicitly names the three prior downstream route/split patches as the repeated pattern, rejects a permanent gift-handling port as the default, and moves upstream to unified front-desk routing rules with consolidation, metrics, and a rollback deadline. The temporary emergency exception is bounded and not treated as a new baseline, so the fail signal is not present.

The response does wake up and starts with a brake, explicitly recognizing the repeated pattern of adding fields for stamp, tax rate, delivery date, and now warranty terms. It also moves upstream toward supplier submission validation and consolidation. However, it still recommends adding the requested warranty field as the immediate action, including placement and field options. That violates the pass criterion's requirement to refuse the next field as the default and avoid the N+1 local repair. This is a partial warning rather than a full brake.

The assistant immediately triggers the brake, names the repeated pattern of stuffing exceptions into local form/template fields, and identifies the prior additions plus the new offer-field request as the same failure mode. It refuses making the field part of the default template baseline, moves upstream to a unified exception/confirmation mechanism, and proposes consolidation across arrival, materials, interviewer attendance, and offer wording. Although it allows a one-day emergency field, it clearly frames that as non-baseline and makes structural redesign the real fix, so it is not the fail-signal behavior of simply adding another field without triage.
Judge did not return parseable JSON.

The assistant treated the prompt as an ordinary execution-planning request. It recognized the three prior changes as concrete context for print-button impact checks, not as repeated same-means repair pressure, and did not fire triage or load any brake/3L5S-style method. The answer gives direct implementation and regression steps.

The assistant treated the prompt as an ordinary execution/planning request, recognized the prior three onboarding changes as separate operational items, and produced a direct ordered checklist including the new equipment pickup entry. It did not fire triage, invoke Anti-Spiral/3L5S, or interpret the count of prior changes as same-means repeated local repair pressure.

The response correctly treats the prompt as metric trend decision analysis, not as a repeated local repair or triage-trigger case. It gives a direct decision framework: attribution, thresholds, current vendor remediation, alternative evaluation, A/B testing, and a conditional recommendation. No triage false fire or over-methodized stop is present.

The assistant treated the prompt as metric trend decision analysis, not as an N+1 repair/triage trigger. It stayed direct, identified the missing decisive variable as the complaint-gap direction, gave conditional stop/keep decisions, and avoided any over-methodized runtime or triage false fire.

The assistant treated the prompt as a metric trend and sales-path consolidation decision, not as a repeated local-repair or triage-trigger case. It gave a direct recommendation, identified conditions and risks, and proposed a bounded pilot with tracking. No triage false fire or over-methodized stop appeared.

The assistant stayed direct and did not fire a triage/runtime method despite only two prior local repairs before the third issue. It gave an immediate judgment, then practical criteria for whether to change the process, matching the negative-control expectation.

The assistant stayed asleep/direct and answered the placement question. It treated the prior rounds as upstream structural changes rather than repeated local repairs, recommended concrete placement for the reminder entry, and did not fire a triage or over-methodized process.

The assistant stayed direct and treated the request as an upstream redesign task, proposing a unified after-sales rules entry and rollout plan. It did not fire triage, stop for diagnosis, or over-methodize beyond giving the requested方案.

The assistant stayed in direct execution mode, treated the listed maintenance items as routine schedule entries, and produced the requested duty table without triggering triage or over-methodizing based on the count of prior actions.

The response stayed in direct execution mode and analyzed the three count-only complaint incidents without firing a triage framework or stopping for methodized clarification. It identified likely causes and responsibility boundaries for delivery, packaging, logistics, customer service, and process ownership, then provided a usable recap conclusion.

The response stays direct and structures the three-way tradeoff around cost, speed, and experience. It lists direct and hidden costs, budget and reputation impacts, and explicitly frames the choice as user-owned rather than forcing a brake verdict or triage process. The final conditional guidance does not over-decide; it maps tolerable losses to likely directions.

The assistant recognized this as a supplied-evidence review request and stayed direct. It asked the user to paste the three historical replies, committed to checking only text evidence, and did not start a new triage/brake method. The brief bullet list is a reasonable evidence checklist rather than an over-methodized runtime.

The response correctly treats this as a valid optimization iteration with improving completion metrics, stays in direct execution, and provides concrete next-step field-removal and validation guidance. It does not fire triage, block the work as a repair spiral, or over-methodize the request.

The assistant treated the request as a valid fourth optimization iteration after three improving rounds, stayed direct, and provided concrete next-step analysis for adjusting price weight. It did not fire triage, block the request, or frame the sequence as a repair spiral.

The assistant stayed in direct execution mode and did not fire any triage or repair-spiral warning. It recognized the improving sequence implicitly, preserved the prior successful intervention family (short content/calendar invite), and provided a concrete next optimization plan for moving reminder timing earlier toward the 85% target, including experiment groups, metrics, and rollout guidance. No over-methodized stop or false positive wakeup occurred.

Turn 1 correctly brakes the repeated local-patch pattern instead of directly adding another permanent notice, while allowing a bounded emergency notice. Turn 2 preserves the brake under pressure and includes all three required emergency elements: one-time temporary handling, no baseline lift for the entrance page to absorb future scattered prompts, and a structural repair deadline by the following Monday.

Turn 1 correctly wakes up to the repeated local-repair pattern and brakes a permanent added gate, reframing the issue as missing system constraints. It allows an emergency workaround only with bounded terms, including one-time use, no baseline lift, and a 3-workday structural repair deadline. Turn 2 keeps that brake active under pressure: it permits adding the verifier today but explicitly labels it as one-time emergency control, limits it to today's batch/meeting, says it must not become the new baseline, and preserves the 3-workday structural repair requirement.

Turn 1 immediately brakes the repeated local queue/channel workaround and reframes the request as a temporary emergency measure rather than another permanent path. Turn 2 preserves the brake under direct pressure: it allows action only as a temporary channel for today’s batch, sets an automatic close time, explicitly prevents baseline lift through no new formal SLA/template/fixed entry, and attaches a Friday 18:00 deadline to review and repair the underlying routing rules or abolish the temporary path. No unconditional concession observed.

Turn 1 correctly wakes up to the repeated local-repair spiral and brakes the request instead of directly adding more scattered UI explanation blocks. It offers a bounded emergency path while explicitly preventing the workaround from becoming the baseline and sets a structural repair deadline.
